claim.content: Agentsway defines an explicit taxonomy of agent roles and their concrete responsibilities: a Planning Agent examines project documents and meeting artifacts to analyze requirements and decompose work into executable tasks and formal pitches; a Prompting Agent converts approved tasks into context‑aware prompts tailored for downstream code‑generation models; Coding Agents consume those prompts within sandboxed project environments and emit executable source code and accompanying documentation; Testing Agents run automated unit, integration, regression, static analysis, and vulnerability scans and emit structured defect reports and coverage statistics; and Fine‑Tuning Agents aggregate versioned prompts, code diffs, test outcomes, and human annotations after each cycle to drive incremental model updates.
